programs.name: MO HealthNet (Medicaid)
programs.what_it_does: Provides health insurance coverage for low-income seniors and disabled individuals, including doctor visits, hospital care, and long-term services. In Missouri, it is administered as MO HealthNet with specific eligibility for seniors and those with disabilities.[1][9]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ and disabled individuals meeting income limits (e.g., single gross monthly income under $1,669 for Expanded Medicaid with no resource limit).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Full health coverage; no resource limit for Expanded Medicaid (AEG); income thresholds vary by category.[1]

programs.name: Structured Family Caregiving Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services (HCBS) waiver to provide long-term care support in home settings instead of institutions. Allows family caregivers to be paid for providing care to eligible individuals.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and disabled individuals qualifying for nursing home level care, with income and asset limits under MO HealthNet rules.
programs.benefit_value: Paid family caregiving services, respite, and other HCBS; can combine with other supports like Alzheimer’s grants up to $1,000 annually.[4]

programs.name: Missouri PACE
programs.what_it_does: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) provides comprehensive medical and social services to help seniors live safely in the community. Coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid benefits in one program.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified for nursing facility level of care, able to live safely in the community with PACE support; must reside in a PACE service area.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care including adult day health, transportation, home care, and inpatient services at no additional cost beyond Medicare/Medicaid premiums.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Program (MSP)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare Part B premiums and may cover deductibles, coinsurance for three levels: QMB, SLMB, QI-1. Helps low-income beneficiaries afford Medicare costs.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries; single gross monthly income < $1,735, resources < $9,430; married < $2,320, resources < $14,130.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Part B premium payment ($174.70/month in 2024); additional cost-sharing for QMB; varies by level (QMB, SLMB, QI).[1]

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card to buy healthy food at approved stores. Helps low-income households afford nutritious meals.[1][2][9]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+ and disabled individuals; income-based eligibility through MO Department of Social Services.
programs.benefit_value: Average monthly benefit ~$200-300 per household; higher for seniors/disabled with no elderly/disabled deduction limits.

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides one-time payments to help with heating bills and crisis assistance for energy shutoffs. Administered through local agencies in Missouri.[1][2][4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors; priority for those 60+, disabled, or with high energy costs.
programs.benefit_value: Grants $200-$1,000+ per household depending on income, household size, and fuel type; crisis aid up to full bill payment.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Improves energy efficiency of low-income homes through insulation, sealing, and efficient equipment installation. Reduces energy costs and health risks from poor housing.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters including seniors 60+; priority for disabled, families with children, and high energy users.
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements valued at $5,000-$10,000 per home; lifetime energy savings often exceed costs.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare, Medicare Advantage, Part D, and savings programs. Helps seniors choose plans and resolve issues.[5][7]
programs.who_its_for: All Missouri Medicare beneficiaries, especially low-income seniors and those with disabilities.
programs.benefit_value: Personalized counseling; average savings $1,000+/year through optimal plan selection and MSP enrollment.

programs.name: Commodity Supplemental Food Program (CSFP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly boxes of nutritious USDA foods to combat hunger. Distributed through local senior centers and agencies.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Income-eligible seniors aged 60+; no disability requirement.
programs.benefit_value: $50-$100+ monthly food value per person; funded ~$471K for MO seniors in 2021.[6]

programs.name: Alzheimer’s Association Caregiver Resource Program
programs.what_it_does: Awards grants to caregivers for respite care, supplies, and training to support those caring for dementia patients at home. Complements HCBS waivers.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of individuals with Alzheimer’s, dementia, or related disorders living at home.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,000 annual grant per caregiver for approved expenses including respite.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid community service work training to unemployed low-income seniors. Helps transition to unsubsidized employment.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income individuals aged 55+ unable to find work.
programs.official_url: https://www.experienceworks.org/ or Missouri AARP Foundation SCSEP
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work ~20 hrs/week; supportive services; avg. $6,000-$8,000 annual earnings.

programs.name: Legal Services of Eastern Missouri (Senior Legal Helpline)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for income-eligible seniors on issues like housing, benefits, abuse, and consumer problems. Operates statewide helplines.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal representation valued at $200-$500/hour; thousands helped annually.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and other long-term care facilities to resolve complaints and protect rights. Operates statewide network.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of long-term care facilities and their families in Missouri.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services; resolves 80%+ of complaints without formal action.

programs.name: Missouri Rx Program (MoRx)
programs.what_it_does: This state-funded program assists Missouri seniors on Medicare Part D with prescription drug costs by covering many common medications. It helps reduce out-of-pocket expenses for eligible low-income individuals through local aging agencies.
programs.who_its_for: Missouri residents aged 65+ or eligible seniors on Medicare Part D meeting state income limits.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific pharmaceutical assistance program supplementing federal Medicare Part D, not available in every state.
programs.benefit_value: Average annual savings of $200 per enrollee (over 50,000 seniors benefited in 2024).

programs.name: Missouri Property Tax Credit (Circuit Breaker)
programs.what_it_does: This program provides refunds on property taxes or rent paid by eligible low-income seniors. It offers financial relief to help seniors afford housing costs in Missouri.
programs.who_its_for: Missouri residents aged 65+ (or 62+ if disabled) with household income under limits (e.g., around $30,000-$40,000 depending on status).
programs.whats_unique: State-funded tax credit tailored to Missouri's low-income seniors and disabled, with specific refund amounts not matching federal programs.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $750 for renters or $1,100 for homeowners (over 100,000 claimed in 2023).

programs.name: Aged and Disabled Waiver (ADW)
programs.what_it_does: This Medicaid waiver delivers home and community-based services to prevent nursing home placement. Services include adult day care, homemaker/chore assistance, home-delivered meals, respite care, and transportation.
programs.who_its_for: Missouri residents aged 63+ who are Medicaid-eligible (MO HealthNet), at risk of institutionalization, and meet functional needs assessment.
programs.whats_unique: Missouri's state-administered HCBS waiver with customized services like chore services and advanced respite, optional beyond federal Medicaid requirements.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; up to 10 hours/day adult day care, 2 meals/day, in-home respite (no fixed dollar amount specified).

programs.name: Senior Independent Living Program (SILP)
programs.what_it_does: This program supports community-based services for seniors to maintain independence at home. It funds personal care and supportive services through state allocations.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Missouri seniors aged 60+ needing assistance to avoid institutionalization.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific program funded via Missouri appropriations, distinct from federal Older Americans Act services.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by need; subject to state budget (historically faced cuts but ongoing).
